title: MIT Explains the 12 Possible Endings for AI
description: This video is based on a book called Life 3.0, where MIT professor Max Tegmark describes 12 possible futures. Some are paradise, some are nightmares. And some look like paradise, until you try to escape.
comment: A fascinating breakdown of possible AI futures

title: Yann LeCun's $1B Bet Against LLMs
description: An alternative vision for AI proposed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun.
comment: Interesting counterpoint to the LLM hype and curious to see if his world model approach pans out.

title: Signs of introspection in large language models
description: Anthropic's research on "introspection" suggests that Large Language Models (specifically Claude Opus 4 and 4.1) can, to a limited degree, monitor and control their own internal neural states.
comment: Wild that we're at the point where models might actually know what they're doing internally.

title: The Hardest Problem AI Ever Solved, with Google DeepMind CEO
description: Cleo Abram interviewing Demis Hassabis to find the future he wants to build
comment: I don't get tired on watching this man speaks. And Cleo makes it even more interesting. Love this interview.

title: Google's Demis Hassabis, Anthropic's Dario Amodei Debate the World After AGI | AI1G
description: At World Economic Forum 2026, leading AI voices debate life after artificial general intelligence. Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google DeepMind; Dario Amodei, CEO of Anthropic; and Zanny Minton Beddoes, Editor-in-Chief of The Economist, discuss AGI risks, governance, and global impact.
comment: Love how they roasted Sam Altman without even saying his name.

title: Contributing to Open Source Can Change Your Life - Here's How to Do It
description: GitHub is the new resume and every contribution you make builds your collaboration skills and associates your name with the massive community of driven individuals out there making software for fun and profit.
comment: I hate my selft for not contributing to open source porjects yet. I must start.
